_documentation.description: Key Vault-integrated Power Automate flow template for Forms to Fabric. Unlike the basic template (flow-template.json), this version reads the Azure Function key from Key Vault at runtime. When keys are rotated, flows automatically use the new key with zero downtime — no manual flow edits required.
key_vault_integration: The flow uses the Azure Key Vault connector (premium) to retrieve the function key at execution time.
Secret 'function-app-key' is read from Key Vault before every HTTP call to the Azure Function.
When 'scripts/rotate_function_key.py' updates the Key Vault secret, running flows pick up the new key automatically.
No flow re-deployment or manual editing is needed after key rotation.
notes: This flow is part of a healthcare Forms to Fabric pipeline.
Clinicians submit responses via Microsoft Forms; this flow triggers processing through an Azure Function that de-identifies PHI before landing data in Fabric Lakehouse.
All PHI handling and de-identification is performed server-side by the Azure Function — the flow itself only transports data.
The Azure Key Vault connector requires a premium Power Automate license.
Ensure the Key Vault access policy grants the Power Automate connection identity GET permission on secrets.

flow_name: Forms to Fabric: Process Response (Key Vault)

trigger.type: When a new response is submitted
trigger._comment_trigger: This trigger fires each time a clinician submits a new response to the configured Microsoft Form.
trigger._comment_connector: Uses the Microsoft Forms connector (standard connector, no premium license required).
trigger._comment_setup: When configuring, the admin must select the target Form from the 'Form Id' dropdown. The Form must be owned by or shared with the connection account.

actions.name: Get_response_details
actions._comment: Retrieves the full response data (all question answers, respondent info, submission timestamp) for the response that triggered the flow. Uses the responseId from the trigger output.

actions.name: Get_secret
actions._comment: Reads the current function app host key from Key Vault at runtime. This is the key step that enables zero-downtime key rotation: when the 'function-app-key' secret is updated in Key Vault (e.g., via rotate_function_key.py), every subsequent flow run automatically picks up the new value without any manual edits.
_setup_notes: 1. In Power Automate, add the 'Get secret' action from the Azure Key Vault connector.
2. Select or create a connection to Key Vault (use a service principal or managed identity with GET secret permissions).
3. Set the vault name to the Key Vault deployed by the Bicep template.
5. The secret value is available in subsequent steps as: body('Get_secret')?['value']

actions.name: Call_Azure_Function
actions._comment: POSTs the form response payload to the Azure Function for processing. The x-functions-key header value is dynamically retrieved from the previous Key Vault step, so it always uses the current key — even after rotation.

actions.name: Check_response_status
actions._comment: Evaluates whether the Azure Function returned HTTP 200 (success). On success, no further action is needed — the function has already de-identified PHI and landed the data in Fabric Lakehouse. On failure, an email alert is sent to the admin with error details for investigation.

if_true._comment: No action required. The Azure Function successfully processed the response, performed PHI de-identification, and wrote the data to the Fabric Lakehouse.

if_false._comment: Sends a failure alert to the configured admin. The email includes the HTTP status code and response body to aid debugging. Ensure the ADMIN_NOTIFICATION_EMAIL parameter is set to a monitored mailbox.

inputs.subject: Forms to Fabric Error: Response @{triggerOutputs()?['body/responseId']}
inputs.body:
Forms to Fabric Error

A form response failed to process.

Form ID: @{triggerOutputs()?['body/formId']}

Response ID: @{triggerOutputs()?['body/responseId']}

Timestamp: @{utcNow()}

HTTP Status Code: @{outputs('Call_Azure_Function')?['statusCode']}

Error Details:

@{body('Call_Azure_Function')}

Please investigate in the Azure portal and resubmit if necessary.

AZURE_FUNCTION_URL.description: The full URL of the Azure Function endpoint (e.g., https://<function-app-name>.azurewebsites.net/api/process-form-response). Obtain from 'azd env get-values' after deployment.

KEY_VAULT_NAME.description: Name of the Azure Key Vault that stores the function app host key (e.g., kv-forms-dev). The Key Vault connector connection must have GET secret permission.

ADMIN_NOTIFICATION_EMAIL.description: Email address that receives pipeline error notifications. Should be a monitored shared mailbox or distribution list (e.g., forms-admin@contoso.com).

FORM_ID.description: The unique identifier of the Microsoft Form used for clinician data collection. Found in the Form URL or the Forms admin portal.

setup_instructions: 1. Deploy the Azure Function and infrastructure using 'azd up'. The Key Vault and placeholder secret are created automatically.
2. Run the rotation script to populate the Key Vault with a real function key:
3. Open Power Automate (https://make.powerautomate.com) and create a new Automated Cloud Flow.
4. Select the 'When a new response is submitted' trigger from the Microsoft Forms connector.
5. In the trigger, select the target clinician Form from the 'Form Id' dropdown.
6. Add a 'Get response details' action (Microsoft Forms connector). Set Form Id and Response Id to reference the trigger outputs.
7. Add a 'Get secret' action from the Azure Key Vault connector. Select the Key Vault and set the secret name to 'function-app-key'.
8. Add an 'HTTP' action. Set method to POST, paste the Azure Function URL, add Content-Type header, and set x-functions-key to the dynamic content: body('Get_secret')?['value'].
9. Configure the JSON body as shown in the template.
10. Add a 'Condition' action to check if the HTTP status code equals 200.
11. In the 'If no' branch, add a 'Send an email (V2)' action (Office 365 Outlook connector) with the error notification template.
12. Save the flow and test with a sample form submission.
13. Future key rotations only require running the rotation script — no flow edits needed.
